channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israel expands its bombardment in Lebanon as thousands flee widening war
item.description: MASNAA BORDER CROSSING, Lebanon (AP) — Israel expanded its bombardment in Lebanon on Saturday, hitting Beirut’s southern suburbs with a dozen airstrikes and striking a Palestinian refugee camp deep in the north for the first time as it targeted both Hezbollah and Hamas fighters.
item.dc:creator: BASSAM HATOUM, MELANIE LIDMAN, BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden pledged to campaign hard for Harris. So far, he's been mostly a no-show
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — On the last day of August, President Joe Biden was asked about his fall campaign plans. He promised a Labor Day appearance in Pittsburgh and said he would be “on the road from there on.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and BILL BARROW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Does a cumulus cloud really weigh a million pounds?
item.description: Just because something floats doesn't mean it is weightless. A great example is when oil is poured onto water. The oil will float on top of the water, but that doesn't mean it has no weight.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Bacca, KRON4

item.title: Russian prosecutors seek 7-year sentence for US man accused of fighting for Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian prosecutors asked for a seven-year sentence in the trial of a U.S. citizen accused of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine against Russia, Russian news agencies reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dallas Uber driver seen on video punching passenger after dispute over legroom
item.description: A 68-year-old Uber passenger was punched in the back of the head in Dallas, Texas, after he complained about the lack of legroom inside a car, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Texas execution is renewing calls for clemency. It's rarely granted
item.description: Texas rarely grants clemency for death row inmates, but that's not stopping a group of bipartisan lawmakers from showing their support for a man scheduled to be executed in October.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan And Summer Ballentine, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bands drop out of Orlando music festival after Kyle Rittenhouse booked as guest
item.description: Shell Shock organizers describe the festival as a concert charity event that seeks to raise funds for a charity that "supports first responders struggling with injuries to include Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: When will the power return? Weary Carolinas residents long for relief after Helene's fury
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — The weary and worn residents of Julianne Johnson's neighborhood in Asheville have been getting by without electricity since Hurricane Helene tore through the Southeast last week and upended their lives.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republican apologizes for wearing blackface at 2006 Halloween party
item.description: Rep. Mike Lawler (R-N.Y.) apologized late Thursday night for wearing blackface as part of a Halloween costume portraying pop star Michael Jackson in 2006 during a college party.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Helene’s powerful storm surge killed 12 near Tampa. They didn't have to die
item.description: INDIAN ROCKS BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Aiden Bowles was stubborn, so even as Florida officials told residents of the barrier island north of St. Petersburg that Hurricane Helene's storm surge could be deadly, the retired restaurant owner stayed put.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER and TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harris to tour Helene damage in North Carolina on Saturday
item.description: Vice President Harris will travel Saturday to North Carolina to tour the damage from Hurricane Helene and get an update on the ongoing recovery efforts in the state.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Need a passport? You can now get one faster
item.description: U.S. passport processing time has been reduced by two weeks at most, the State Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: For Pittsburgh Jews, attack anniversary adds to an already grim October
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Jewish communities everywhere reacted with horror at last year's Oct. 7 attack by Hamas on Israel, but the approaching one-year commemoration of the assault hits home particularly hard in Pittsburgh's Jewish community, which already marks a grim anniversary each October.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden student loan cancellation plan on hold again
item.description: The Biden administration’s student loan cancellation plan has been put on hold once again in Missouri after brief celebration from advocates this week when another judge ruled the plan could move forward.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Immigrant murder convictions 'tens of thousands' higher than ICE's bombshell figures: data expert
item.description: The total number of noncitizens in the U.S. who have murder convictions is likely "tens of thousands" more than the 13,400 listed on Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s national docket.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a “listening device” in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Garth Brooks responds to sexual assault allegations
item.description: Country music superstar Garth Brooks has issued a statement following a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, ABC 4

item.title: Menendez brothers trial juror: 'It was a battle of the sexes'
item.description: "The men did not believe that Jose (Menendez) had been abusing his sons," Thornton said Thursday on "Banfield.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon woman gets probation after freezing puppies to feed snake, gets to keep pets
item.description: An Oregon woman was sentenced on Wednesday on several animal abuse charges after authorities learned she froze several litters of puppies to feed her pet snakes.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, Fox 5

item.title: Eight city workers arrested in Columbus Animal Care & Control investigation
item.description: "This investigation also uncovered multiple criminal violations that are part of the Georgia State Code," Deputy Police Chief Lance Deaton said.
item.dc:creator: Hannah James, NewsNation

item.title: Rhode Island woman works 350 hours to build first home
item.description: "It makes you excited to think that you're going to build your own home and help others to get theirs," she told NewsNatoin affiliate WPRI. "That's like a dream come true.
item.dc:creator: Catie McNeil, NewsNation

item.title: Man accused of trying to carjack pregnant woman in Connecticut drive-thru
item.description: With their help, police were able to arrest the suspect, who police say is facing a multitude of charges in this case and others.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports reached a deal Thursday to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15 to allow time to negotiate new contract
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports has reached a deal to suspend their strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract, a person briefed on the matter says.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother are youngest-known Helene victims
item.description: A large tree had crashed through the roof, crushing Kobe Williams and causing her to fall on top of infant sons Khyzier and Khazmir. All three were found dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, Fox 5

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks accused of sexual assault by makeup artist
item.description: Garth Brooks, the country music superstar who gained fame in the 1990s with several hit songs, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Most visited national park site closed indefinitely due to Hurricane Helene
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to ‘reevaluate’ Menendez brothers’ convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón provided an update Thursday on the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in August 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Deadly Maui fire erupted from earlier blaze believed to have been extinguished, investigation finds
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — The wildfire that killed at least 102 people on Maui last year erupted from an earlier brushfire, sparked by downed power lines, that firefighters believed they had extinguished, officials confirmed Wednesday as they presented their findings on the cause. The Aug.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Doctor who helped supply Matthew Perry ketamine pleads guilty
item.description: A San Diego doctor charged in connection with Matthew Perry’s fatal overdose pleaded guilty Wednesday to conspiring to distribute the surgical anesthetic ketamine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Are LGBTQ Jews welcome in Orthodox communities? This is how they are building spaces of their own
item.description: It was a heart-wrenching choice. But when Daniel Gammerman decided to never set foot back in an Orthodox synagogue, he thought of it as an act of love. Not toward the Jewish community he was born into, but to himself.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Leslie forms; Hurricane Kirk strengthens
item.description: Hurricane Kirk is expected to grow larger as it strengthens in the Atlantic, the National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Explainer: How immigrants end up on ICE's non-detained docket
item.description: Recent reports that over 435,000 convicted criminals are on U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement's non-detained docket (NDD) -- meaning they are not detained by the agency while they await immigration proceedings -- has spurred much criticism and confusion.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: Vance, Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees J.D. Vance and Tim Walz are facing off for the first -- and likely only -- time before the 2024 election, which is just over a month away.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets,’ with a new color
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween, and a new color has joined the lineup this year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Ken Page, who starred in 'Cats' on Broadway and voiced Oogie Boogie, dies at 70
item.description: Ken Page, a stage and screen actor who starred alongside Beyoncé in “Dreamgirls,” introduced Broadway audiences to Old Deuteronomy in “Cats” and scared generations of kids as the voice of Oogie Boogie, the villain of the 1993 animated holiday film ”The Nightmare Before Christmas,″ has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
